Commands verified: 'maestro mission create --file <plan.json> [--json]', 'maestro milestone list --mission <mission-id>', 'maestro milestone seal <milestone-name> --mission <mission-id>', 'maestro feature list --mission <mission-id>', 'maestro feature show <feature-id> --mission <mission-id>', 'maestro feature approve <feature-id> --mission <mission-id>', 'maestro feature prompt <feature-id> --mission <mission-id> --out <path>' - all consistent with CLI patterns
Commands verified: 'maestro mission create', 'maestro feature approve', 'maestro validation show' - consistent with CLI patterns
Commands verified: 'maestro validation show', 'maestro feature list --mission <id> --milestone <name>' - consistent with CLI patterns

Commands verified: 'maestro validation show', 'maestro feature list --mission <id> --milestone <name>', 'maestro validation update --milestone <name> --status <passed|failed>', 'maestro milestone seal', 'maestro checkpoint save' - all consistent with CLI patterns

Commands verified: 'maestro validation show', 'maestro feature prompt <featureId> --mission <id> --out <path>', 'maestro validation update --milestone <name> --status <passed|failed>', 'maestro checkpoint save', 'maestro checkpoint list' - all consistent with CLI patterns

evidence.frontmatterValidation: All five files have valid YAML frontmatter with --- delimiters containing name, description, and argument-hint fields
evidence.commandReferences: Spot-checked command references across all five skills - all consistent with CLI syntax patterns using 'maestro <noun> <verb> [--flag <value>]' structure

summary: Tested 1 assertion: VAL-SKILLS-001 passed. All five built-in Mission Control skill files exist with valid YAML frontmatter and command references consistent with shipped CLI syntax.
